text: When setting project goals, it is helpful to use the SMART framework. SMART stands for Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," a SMART goal would be "increase website traffic by 20% within three months." To track progress, break your goal into smaller milestones. Each milestone represents a key step, such as completing a design prototype. At each milestone, you should produce a deliverable, which is a tangible result like a report or a new feature. Finally, stakeholder alignment is crucial. This means ensuring everyone involved, from team members to clients, agrees on the goals, milestones, and deliverables before you begin.
